windows-gfx1101.blocked_reason: Never Windows-ported (even for CUDA): zero _WIN32 guards in csrc/. The ROCm/HIP GPU kernels hipify cleanly (0 unsupported CUDA calls), but csrc/mem_alloc.cpp (the c_ops host allocator) includes <linux/mman.h>/<linux/mempolicy.h> and uses mmap/NUMA/hugepage/shm pinned allocators with no Windows equivalent. First build error: bitmap.cpp __builtin_popcount/__builtin_ctz undefined in MSVC. Windows GPU validation requires a full Win32 host-layer port (VirtualAlloc+large-pages+Win32-shared-memory) that is separate from and out of scope for the ROCm port. Same root cause as gfx1151 block. gfx90a/gfx1100 (Linux) validated.

windows-gfx1201.blocked_reason: Host-wide block, GPU-independent (carried from windows-gfx1101 determination): host C++ layer uses Linux-only POSIX APIs (linux/mman.h, mmap, shm_open, NUMA) with no Windows path; never ported to Windows even for CUDA. GPU kernel surface hipifies cleanly. Identical compile-time failure on gfx1201 (same Windows host/toolchain), nothing GPU-specific to validate.

windows-gfx1151.blocked_reason: Never Windows-ported (even for CUDA): zero _WIN32 guards in csrc/. The ROCm/HIP GPU kernels hipify cleanly, but the c_ops host allocator (csrc/mem_alloc.cpp: POSIX sys/mman/unistd mmap+hugepage+shm pinned allocators, called by memory_management.py) and storage backends (redis/event_notifier: unistd.h/eventfd) have no Windows port. Validating on Windows needs a POSIX->Win32 host porting effort (allocators + networking) SEPARATE from the ROCm port and out of scope. Not a ROCm/gfx1151 defect (gfx90a/gfx1100 pass). Partial Windows fixes in windows-partial-fixes.patch.
